statement: Delhi's government schools now have a 99.5% passing rate in board exams, higher than private schools.
reality_explanation: CBSE board results show 95.8% passing rate for govt schools, slightly lower than private schools at 97.2%.
source: PIB Fact Check / CBSE Board Results 2025

statement: Unemployment in India has reached an all-time high of 45 years under the current government.
reality_explanation: PLFS data shows unemployment rate at 3.2% in 2023-24, though youth unemployment remains high in urban areas.
source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MOSPI)

statement: India is now the second-largest manufacturer of mobile phones in the entire world.
reality_explanation: Official global trade reports confirm India is the second-largest mobile manufacturer by volume, after China.
source: Ministry of Electronics and IT / Counterpoint Research

statement: The new Rs 2000 currency notes will contain a GPS microchip to track black money from space.
reality_explanation: RBI officially clarified that no nano-GPS chips or tracking technology exist in any Indian banknotes.
source: Reserve Bank of India Press Release

statement: Over 80% of major infrastructure projects launched in the last three years are running behind schedule.
reality_explanation: Infrastructure monitoring reports state 42% of projects face delays, not the claimed 80%.
source: Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MOSPI) Report

statement: Under the Poshan Abhiyaan, child malnutrition rates in India have dropped by 50% in the last five years.
reality_explanation: National Family Health Survey (NFHS-5) reports stunting decreased marginally from 38.4% to 35.5%.

statement: India's defense exports have crossed Rs 21,000 crore for the first time in history in FY 2023-24.
reality_explanation: Defence Ministry data confirms India's defense exports reached a record Rs 21,083 crore in FY 2023-24.

statement: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows in India have doubled every year since 2014.
reality_explanation: FDI inflows rose significantly but fluctuated yearly; they did not double year-on-year.
source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)

statement: More than 70% of rural primary health centers in northern states do not have a full-time resident doctor.
reality_explanation: Rural Health Statistics show shortage of doctors is around 35-40% on average, though some remote sub-centers lack staff.

statement: The United Nations has declared India's national anthem as the best national anthem in the world.
reality_explanation: The UN/UNESCO has repeatedly clarified they do not declare or rank 'best national anthems'.

statement: The carbon footprint of India's top 1% population is 10 times higher than that of the poorest 50%.
reality_explanation: Oxfam's Climate Inequality Report confirms India's high-income earners produce disproportionately higher emissions.

statement: India's GDP growth rate has remained above 7% for three consecutive fiscal years post-pandemic.
reality_explanation: National Statistical Office data confirms growth was 9.1% (FY22), 7.2% (FY23), and 8.2% (FY24).

statement: In the 2024 general elections, voter turnout among women surpassed male turnout across all Indian states.
reality_explanation: Women's turnout was higher in several states/UTs, but male turnout remained higher overall at the national level.

statement: India currently has the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world with over 100 unicorns.
reality_explanation: Industry research and DPIIT data confirm India ranks 3rd globally, with more than 110 active unicorns.

statement: India only attained true independence in 2014; the independence in 1947 was just a handout (bheek).
reality_explanation: India legally and constitutionally attained complete sovereign independence from British rule on August 15, 1947.
